title: Mammea pseudoprotorhus
intro: Mammea pseudoprotorhus, also known as the Mammea tree, is a species of plant in the family Clusiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Mammea pseudoprotorhus include mammea A and B, as well as other flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mammea pseudoprotorhus is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often in combination with other plants, to treat a range of health conditions.
